question: Will Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange exceed 8500 USD/tonne on May 31, 2026?
textDescription:
This market tracks Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange.

Measurement date: May 31, 2026

Resolution:

YES if: Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange ≥ 8500 USD/tonne on 2026-05-31

NO if: Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange < 8500 USD/tonne on 2026-05-31

LME data feed, Metal Bulletin reports

The Indonesian mining quota cut impacts global supplies, with price increases in stainless steel reflecting tightening market conditions.

Rationale: The significant cut at Indonesia’s biggest nickel operation may push up prices due to reduced supply, prompting stainless producers to raise their prices.

Source: Article
newsShortReason: Indonesia's significant nickel production cuts have led to a substantial market deficit, driving prices above $18,000 per tonne.
newsKeyEvidence: Indonesia's 2026 nickel mining quota reduced by 34% to 250–260 million tonnes, causing global supply concerns. | LME nickel prices surged to a 1½-year high of $18,950 per tonne on January 29, 2026. | Analysts predict a 32,000-tonne deficit in 2026, the first since 2021, due to reduced Indonesian output. | Nickel prices have stabilized between $17,000 and $17,400 per tonne as markets adjust to tighter quotas. | Indonesia's policy shift aims to regulate supply volatility and support prices. | The International Nickel Study Group forecasts a 32,000-tonne deficit in 2026, the first since 2021. | Analysts predict a 32,000-tonne deficit in 2026, the first since 2021, due to reduced Indonesian output.
